title: Persist Data Mappings Across Logout/Login Sessions
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want my data mappings to persist after logging out and logging back in, so that I don't lose my mapping configuration between sessions.
description:
Data mappings were lost when a user logged out and logged back in. Closing and reopening the browser window preserved mappings (stored in DOM), but a full logout/login cycle did not.

### Root Cause
Mappings were managed entirely in the DOM (client-side) rather than persisted to the database.

### Fix
Migrated mapping state from DOM-only storage to database persistence, ensuring mappings survive logout/login cycles.
acceptance_criteria: Data mappings persist after logout and login
Closing and reopening the browser window still preserves mappings
Previously saved mappings are recalled correctly after re-authentication
ux_changes: Mappings now load from database on page load instead of relying on DOM state, ensuring they appear after logout/login.
middleware_changes: Added database read/write operations for mapping state that previously lived only in the DOM.
data_model_changes: Mapping state stored in database instead of DOM-only. New or updated table/columns to persist user mapping configurations.
implementation_notes: Migrated from DOM-based mapping state to database persistence. QA confirmed all existing functionality works correctly after the change.
comment_summary: Waqar identified the issue as DOM-only storage and implemented database persistence. Ryan tested and confirmed it worked. Waqar verified full QA and asked to close the ticket.
